questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $S$ represents a circle of minimum radius in Argand plane which passes through $z_1$ and $z_2$, then
options: Centre of $S$ is represented by $\left(z_1+2 z_2\right)$
Let $z$ be any complex number lying on circle $S$ then $\frac{\left(z_2-z\right)}{\left|z_2-z\right|}= \pm \frac{\left(z-z_1\right)}{\left|z-z_1\right|}$ i
questions.solution: $z_1$ and $z_2$ are end points of diameter.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $z_1, z_2, z_3, z_4$ are roots of the equation $a_0 z^4+a_1 z^3+a_2 z^2+a_3 z+a_4=0$, where $a_0, a_1, a_2, a_3$ and $a_4$ are real, then
options: $\overline{\mathbf{z}}_1, \overline{\mathbf{z}}_2, \overline{\mathbf{z}}_3, \overline{\mathbf{z}}_4$ are also roots of the equation
$\mathbf{z}_1$ is equal to at least one of $\overline{\mathbf{z}}_1, \overline{\mathbf{z}}_2, \overline{\mathbf{z}}_3, \overline{\mathbf{z}}_4$
$-\overline{\mathbf{z}}_1,-\overline{\mathbf{z}}_2,-\overline{\mathbf{z}}_3,-\overline{\mathbf{z}}_4$ are also roots of the equation
questions.solution:
$a_0 z^4+a_1 z^3+a_2 z^2+a_3 z+a_4=0$
Taking conjugate on both sides.
$a_0(\bar{z})^4+a_1(\bar{z})^3+a_2(\bar{z})^2+a_3 \bar{z}+a_4=0$
$\therefore \quad \overline{\mathbf{Z}}_1, \overline{\mathbf{z}}_2, \overline{\mathbf{z}}_3, \overline{\mathbf{z}}_4$ are the roots of the equation if $\mathbf{z}_1$ is real, then $\overline{\mathbf{Z}}_1$ is also real and if $\mathbf{z}_1$ is non real, then $\overline{\mathbf{Z}}_1$ is also root because imaginary roots occur in conjugate pair.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $x, y, a, b$ are real numbers such that $(x+i y)^{1 / 5}=a+i b$ and $p=\frac{x}{a}-\frac{y}{b}$, then
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & (x+i y)^{\frac{1}{5}}=a+i b \\ & \therefore x+i y=(a+i b)^5=a^5+5 a^4(i b)+10 a^3\left(-b^2\right)+10 a^2\left(-i b^3\right)+5 a b^4+i b^5\end{aligned}$
Equating the real and imaginary parts $x=a^5-10 a^3 b^2+5 a b^4 \Rightarrow y=5 a^4 b-10 a^2 b^3+b^5$
$p=\frac{x}{a}-\frac{y}{b}=\left(a^4-10 a^2 b^2+5 b^4\right)-\left(5 a^4-10 a^2 b^2+b^4\right)=-4 a^4+4 b^4=-4\left(a^4-b^4\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & =-4\left(a^2-b^2\right)\left(a^2+b^2\right)=-4(a+b)(a-b)(a+i b)(a-i b) \\ & \therefore a-b, a+b, a+i b \text { and } a-i b \text { are all factors of } p .\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The all possible order pairs $(a, b)$ that satisfy the relation
$(4-3 i) a^2+(3+2 i) a b=4 b^2-\frac{a^2}{2}+\left(3 a b-2 b^2\right) i$ is/are
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & (3 a+4 b)(3 a-2 b)=0 \text { and }(3 a-2 b)(a+b)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow 3 a=2 b\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Suppose that the complex number $z$ lies on the curve such that $\frac{z-4}{z-2 i}$ is purely imaginary. If the complex number $z_1$ represents the mid-point of chord OA of this curve, $O$ being the origin, then $z_1$ necessarily satisfies
questions.solution:
$\frac{z-4}{z-2 i}+\frac{\bar{z}-4}{\bar{z}+2 i}=0$ which is a circle $x^2+y^2-4 x-2 y=0$
$\mathrm{xx}_1+y \mathrm{y}_1-2\left(x+x_1\right)-\left(y+y_1\right)=x_1^2+y_1^2-4 x_1-2 y_1$

It passes through $(0,0)$, so, the locus of $\left(x_1, y_1\right)$ is $x^2+y^2-2 x-y=0$.
So, $z_1=x_1+i y_1$, lies on this circle for which the points $(2,0)$ and $(0,1)$ are extremities of a diameter. Also $(0,0)$ and $(2,1)$ represent extremities of another diameter
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $a, b, c$ be distinct complex numbers with $|a|=|b|=|c|=1$ and $z_1, z_2$ be the roots of the equation $a z^2+b z+c=0$ with $\left|z_1\right|=1$. Also let $P$ and $Q$ represents the complex numbers $z_1$ and $\mathrm{z}_2$ in the complex plane with $\angle \mathrm{POQ}=\theta$ where $O$ is the origin then
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \left|z_1\right|=\left|z_2\right|=1 ;\left|z_1+z_2\right|=1 \\ & \Rightarrow\left(z_1+z_2\right)\left(\bar{z}_1+\bar{z}_2\right)=1\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \left|z_1+z_2\right|=\left|z_1\right|\left|1+e^{i \theta}\right|=1 \\ & \left|z_2-z_1\right|=\left|z_1\right|\left|e^{i \theta}-1\right|\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let ' $z$ ' be a complex number and ' $a$ ' be a real parameter such that $z^2+a z+a^2=0$, then
options: locus of $z$ is a pair of straight lines
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & z^2+a z+a^2=0 \\ & \Rightarrow z=a \omega, a \omega^2 \text { (where ' } \omega \text { ' is non real root of unity) } \\ & \Rightarrow \operatorname{locus} \text { of } z \text { is a pair of straight lines and } \arg (z)=\arg (a)+\arg (\omega) \operatorname{or} \arg (a)+\arg \left(\omega^2\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \arg (z)= \pm \frac{2 \pi}{3} \text { also, }|z|=|a||\omega| \text { or }|a|\left|\omega^2\right| \Rightarrow|z|=|a|\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Equation of a straight line on the complex plane passing through a point $P$ denoting the complex number $\alpha$ and perpendicular to the vector $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{OP}}$ where 'O' is the origin can be written as
questions.solution:
Required line is passing through $\mathrm{P}(\alpha)$ and parallel to the vector $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{OQ}}$ hence $z=\alpha+i \lambda \alpha, \lambda \in \mathbb{R}$ $\frac{z-\alpha}{\alpha}=\text { purely imaginary }$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \operatorname{Re}\left(\frac{z-\alpha}{\alpha}\right)=0 \Rightarrow \text { (B) } \mathrm{(multiply} \mathrm{N^r} \mathrm{and} \mathrm{D^r} \text{by} \bar{\alpha} )\\ & \Rightarrow \operatorname{Re}((z-\alpha) \bar{\alpha})=0 \Rightarrow \operatorname{Re}(z \bar{\alpha}-|\bar{\alpha}|)=0 \\ & \text { also, } \frac{z-\alpha}{\alpha}+\frac{\bar{z}-\bar{\alpha}}{\bar{\alpha}}=0 \\ & \bar{\alpha}(z-\alpha)+\alpha(\bar{z}-\bar{\alpha})=0 \\ & \bar{\alpha} z+\alpha \bar{z}-2|\alpha|^2=0 \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $z_1, z_2, z_3$ be three complex number such that $\left|z_1\right|=\left|z_2\right|=\left|z_3\right|=1$ and $\frac{z_1^2}{z_2 z_3}+\frac{z_2^2}{z_1 z_3}+\frac{z_3^2}{z_1 z_2}+1=0$
then $\left|z_1+z_2+z_3\right|$ can take the value equal to
questions.solution:
Given $\left|z_1\right|=\left|z_2\right|=\left|z_3\right|=1$
$\Rightarrow z_1=\frac{1}{\bar{z}_1}$ etc.
also $\frac{z_1^2}{z_2 z_3}+\frac{z_2^2}{z_1 z_3}+\frac{z_3^2}{z_1 z_2}+1=0$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow\left(z_1\right)^3+\left(z_2\right)^3+\left(z_3\right)^3+z_1 z_2 z_3=0 \\ & \Rightarrow\left(z_1\right)^3+\left(z_2\right)^3+\left(z_3\right)^3-3 z_1 z_2 z_3=-4 z_1 z_2 z_3\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \left(z_1+z_2+z_3\right)\left[\left(z_1\right)^2+\left(z_2\right)^2+\left(z_3\right)^2-\sum z_1 z_2\right]=-4 z_1 z_2 z_3 \\ & \sum z_1\left[\left(\sum z_1\right)^2-3 \sum z_1 z_2\right]=-4 z_1 z_2 z_3\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { let } z_1+z_2+z_3=z \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \bar{z}_1+\bar{z}_2+\bar{z}_3=\bar{z}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{z}\left[\mathrm{z}^2-3 \sum \mathrm{z}_1 \mathrm{z}_2\right]=-4 \mathrm{z}_1 \mathrm{z}_2 \mathrm{z}_3 \\ & \mathrm{z}^3=3 \mathrm{z} \sum \mathrm{z}_1 \mathrm{z}_2-4 \mathrm{z}_1 \mathrm{z}_2 \mathrm{z}_3\end{aligned}$
$z^3=z_1 z_2 z_3\left[3 z\left(\frac{1}{z_1}+\frac{1}{z_2}+\frac{1}{z_3}\right)-4\right]=z_1 z_2 z_3\left[3 z\left(\bar{z}_1+\bar{z}_2+\bar{z}_3\right)-4\right]$
$z^3=z_1 z_2 z_3\left[3|z|^2-4\right]$
$\therefore \quad|z|^3=\left.|3| z\right|^2-4 \mid$ .....(1)
Now, if $|z| \geq \frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}$
Then $|z|^3=3|z|^2-4$
$\Rightarrow|z|^3-3|z|^2+4=0$
$\Rightarrow|z|^2(|z|-2)-|z|(|z|-2)-2(|z|-2)=0$
$\Rightarrow(|z|-2)\left(|z|^2-|z|-2\right)=0$
$\Rightarrow(|z|-2)(|z|-2)(|z|+1)=0$
$\Rightarrow|z|=2$ or $|z|=-1$ (rejected)
Hence $|z|=\{1,2\}$ where $|z|=\left|z_1+z_2+z_3\right|$
NOTE: $z_1=1 ; z_2=i$ and $z_3=-i$ and $z_1=1 ; z_2=-w$ and $z_3=w^2$ also gives the result
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For complex numbers $z$ and $w$, if $|z|^2 w-|w|^2 z=z-w$. Which of the following are true :
questions.solution:
Given that $z$ and $\omega$ are two complex numbers. To prove
$|z|^2 w-|w|^2 z=z-w \Leftrightarrow z=w$ or $z \bar{w}=1$
First let us consider
$|z|^2 w-|w|^2 z=z-w$.....(1)
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow z\left(1+|w|^2\right)=w\left(1+|z|^2\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{z}{w}=\frac{1+|z|^2}{1+|w|^2} \\ & \Rightarrow \overline{\left(\frac{z}{w}\right)}=\frac{z}{w} \Rightarrow \frac{\bar{z}}{w}=\frac{z}{w}\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \bar{z} w=z \bar{w}$ .....(2)
Again from Eq. (1),
$\begin{aligned}
& z \bar{z} w-w \bar{w} z=z-w \\
& z(\bar{z} w-1)-w(\bar{w} z-1)=0 \\
& z(z \bar{w}-1)-w(z \bar{w}-1)=0 \text { [Using Eq. (2)] } \\
& \Rightarrow(z \bar{w}-1)(z-w)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow z \bar{w}=1 \text { or } z=w
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $z$ is a complex number which simultaneously satisfies the equations $3|z-12|=5|z-8 i|$ and $|z-4|=\mid z$ $-8 \mid$ then the $\operatorname{Im}(z)$ can be
questions.solution:
Let $z=x+i y$
from $2^{\text {nd }}$ equation $x=6$ put in (1)
$\begin{aligned}
& 3|(x-12)+y i|=5|x+(y-8) i| \\
& 9\left[36+y^2\right]=25\left[36+(y-8)^2\right] \qquad \text { (substituting } x=6)\\
& 9 \cdot 36+9 y^2=25 \cdot 36+25\left[y^2+64-16 y\right] \\
& 16 y^2-25 \cdot 16 y+36 \cdot 16+25 \cdot 64=0 \\
& y^2-25 y+36+100=0 \\
& y^2-25 y+136=0 \\
& (y-17)(y-8)=0
\end{aligned}$
then, $y=17$ or $y=8$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider the locus of the complex number $z$ in the Argand plane is given by $\operatorname{Re}(z)-2=|z-7+2 i|$. Let $P\left(z_1\right)$ and $Q\left(z_2\right)$ be two complex number satisfying the given locus and also satisfying $\arg \left(\frac{z_1-(2+\alpha i)}{z_2-(2+\alpha i)}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}(\alpha \in R)$ then the minimum value of $P Q$ is divisible by
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & z=x+i y \\ & (x-2)^2=(x-7)^2+(y+2)^2 \\ & (x-2)^2-(x-7)^2=(y+2)^2\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & (y+2)^2=5(2 x-9) \\ & (y+2)^2=10\left(x-\frac{9}{2}\right) \\ & Y^2=4 a X \Rightarrow a=\frac{5}{2} \\ & \left.P Q\right|_{\min }=L(L R)=10\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider the equation $z^2-(3+i) z+(m+2 i)=0$ where $m \in R$. If the equation has exactly one real and one non real complex root, then which of the following hold(s) good?
options: Modulus of the non real complex root is 2 .
Additive inverse of non real root is $(-1-i)$.
Product of the real root and imaginary part of non real complex root is 2 .
questions.solution:
If $\alpha$ is a real root then
$\begin{aligned}
& \alpha^2-(3+i) \alpha+(m+2 i)=0 \\
& \left(\alpha^2-3 \alpha+m\right)+i(2-\alpha) \\
& \alpha=2 \text { and } m=+2
\end{aligned}$
Now product of the root $=m+2 i=2(1+i)$ [from the given equation]
As real root is 2
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \quad \text { non real root }=1+\mathrm{i} \\
& \text { additive inverse }=-1-\mathrm{i}
\end{aligned}$
(real root)(imaginary part of complex root)
$2(1)=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\mathrm{z}_1, \mathrm{z}_2, \mathrm{z}_3$ are the vertices of an equilateral triangle with centroid at origin \& length of circum radius of triangle is 1 unit. Then which of the following may be the vertices of an equilateral triangle -
questions.solution:
For equilateral $\Delta$ $\mathrm{z}_1^2+\mathrm{z}_2^2+\mathrm{z}_3^2=\mathrm{z}_1 \mathrm{z}_2+\mathrm{z}_2 \mathrm{z}_3+\mathrm{z}_3 \mathrm{z}_1$ (A) Which is satisfied by $-z_1-z_2 \& z_3$ also similarly $\left|z_1\right|=\left|z_2\right|=\left|z_3\right|=1$ (B) $\mathrm{z}_1+1, \mathrm{z}_2+1, \mathrm{z}_3+1$ are also satisfying the relation (C)
are also vertices of equilateral $\Delta$. (D) $\frac{\mathrm{z}_1}{2}, \frac{\mathrm{z}_2}{2}, \frac{\mathrm{z}_3}{2}$ are also vertices of an equilateral triangle.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the complex number $z$ satisfies the condition $\left|z-\frac{25}{z}\right|=24$, then which of the following is(are) correct?
options: Maximum distance of $z$ from origin is 5
Maximum distance of $z$ from origin is 25
Minimum distance of $z$ from origin is 1
Minimum distance of $z$ from origin is 4
questions.solution:
$\because \quad|| z_1|-| z_2|| \leq\left|z_1+z_2\right|$
$\Rightarrow|| z\left|-\frac{25}{|z|}\right| \leq 24 \quad \Rightarrow-24 \leq|z|-\frac{25}{|z|} \leq 24$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { (i) }|z|^2-24|z|-25 \leq 0 \\ & \Rightarrow(|z|-25)(|z|+1) \leq 0 \Rightarrow|z| \leq 25 \\ & \text { (ii) }|z|^2+24|z|-25 \geq 0 \Rightarrow(|z|+25)(|z|-1) \geq 0 \Rightarrow|z| \geq 1 \\ & \quad \therefore \quad 1 \leq|z| \leq 25\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $a, b, c$ be distinct complex numbers with $|a|=|b|=|c|=1$ and $z_1, z_2$ be the roots of the equation $a z^2+b z+c=0$ with $\left|z_1\right|=1$. Also $P$ and $Q$ are the points representing the complex numbers $z_1$ and $z_2$ respectively in the complex plane with $\angle P O Q=\theta$ (where $O$ being the origin) then which of the following is/are correct?
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { }\left|z_1 z_2\right|=\left|\frac{c}{a}\right|=1, \\ & \left|z_1+z_2\right|=\left|\frac{-b}{a}\right|=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(z_1+z_2\right) \times\left(\bar{z}_1+\bar{z}_2\right)=1 \quad \Rightarrow \quad\left(z_1+z_2\right)\left(\frac{1}{z_2}+\frac{1}{z_2}\right)=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(z_1+z_2\right)^2=z_1 z_2 \\ & \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad\left(\frac{-b}{a}\right)^2=\frac{c}{a} \quad \Rightarrow \quad b^2=a c \\ &\left|z_1+z_2\right|=\left|z_1\right|\left|1+e^{i \theta}\right|=2 \cos \frac{\theta}{2}=1 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \theta=\frac{2 \pi}{3} \\ & P Q=\left|z_2-z_1\right|=\left|z_1\right|\left|e^{i \theta}-1\right|=2 \sin \frac{\theta}{2} \\ &=2 \sin \frac{\pi}{3}=\sqrt{3}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $z$ satisfies $z \bar{z}+(-4+5 i) \bar{z}+(-4-5 i) z-40=0$. If $a=\max .|z+2-3 i|$ and $b=\min .|z+2-3 i|$, then:
questions.solution:
Centre of circle $=(4,-5)$
$$
\text { Radius }=\sqrt{|-4+5 i|^2-(-40)}=\sqrt{4^2+5^2+40}=9
$$
Distance of centre $(4,-5)$ from $(-2,3)$ is 10 .
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad a & =\max .|z-(-2+3 i)|=9+10=19 \\
b & =\min .|z-(-2+3 i)|=10-9=1
\end{aligned}
$$
Now, verify.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the complex numbers $z_1, z_2, z_3 \& z_4$ taken in that order are the vertices of a rhombus, then -
questions.solution: As it is a rhombus $\left|z_1-z_2\right|=\left|z_2-z_3\right|$ $\& \quad \mathrm{z}_1+\mathrm{z}_3=\mathrm{z}_2+\mathrm{z}_4$ $\because$ Diagonals of rhombus are perpendicular bisectors $\Rightarrow \arg \frac{z_1-z_3}{z_2-z_4}= \pm \frac{\pi}{2}$ $\Rightarrow \frac{z_1-z_3}{z_2-z_4}$ is purely imaginary.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For the equation $(2+3 i) z+(2-3 i) \bar{z}-6=0$, which of the following is true -
options: The complex number with minimum modulus satisfying the given equation is $\frac{6}{13}-\frac{9}{13} \mathrm{i}$.
The argument of any complex number satisfying the given equation is $\tan ^{-1} \frac{2}{3}$.
Any $z$ satisfying the given equation also satisfies $z=(3+i)+\lambda(3+2 i)$ for suitable $\lambda \in R$.

If $z_1 \& z_2$ are the two points satisfying given equation such that $\operatorname{Re}\left(z_1\right)=0 \& \operatorname{Im}\left(z_2\right)=0$, then $\frac{1}{2}\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right|=\frac{3}{4}$.
questions.solution: Put $z=x+i y$ $(2+3 i)(x+i y)+(2-3 i)(x-i y)-6=0$ $2(2 x-3 y)-6=0$ $2 x-3 y=3$ Option A : In figure P represents the complex number with minimum modulus. Equation of OP is $y=-\frac{3}{2} x$ Solving OP with AB , we get $\frac{6}{13}-\frac{9}{13}$ i. Option B is incorrect. Option C : $\frac{z-(3+i)}{3+2 i}$ is purely real $\Rightarrow \frac{z-(3+i)}{3+2 i}=\frac{\bar{z}-(3-i)}{3-2 i}$ only solving it gives same line. Option D : $\frac{1}{2}\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right|=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{3}{2}\cdot=\frac{3}{4}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $z$ is the complex number satisfying $|z-3-4 i| \leq 10$ and $\alpha=\sin ^{-1}\left(\sin \left(|z|_{\max }\right)\right.$, $\beta=\cos ^{-1}\left(\cos \left(-\frac{|z|_{\max }+|z|_{\min }}{3}\right)\right)$, then -
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & |z-3-4 i| \leq 10 \\ & |z|_{\max }=15 \\ & |z|_{\min }=0 \\ & \alpha=\sin ^{-1}\left(\sin \left(|z|_{\max }\right)\right)\end{aligned}$ $=\sin ^{-1}(\sin 15)=5 \pi-15$ $\beta=\cos ^{-1}\left(\cos \left(-\frac{|z|_{\max }+|z|_{\min }}{3}\right)\right)$ $=\cos ^{-1} \cos (-5)=\cos ^{-1} \cos 5=2 \pi-5$ $\therefore \alpha-3 \beta=5 \pi-15-6 \pi+15=-\pi$ $\sin ^2 \alpha+\cos ^2 3 \beta=\sin ^2 15+\cos ^2 15=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\omega$ is the imaginary cube root of unity such that $\left|\left(\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}}\left(\mathrm{r} \cdot \sum_{\mathrm{p}=1}^{\mathrm{r}}\left(\omega^{\mathrm{P}-1}\right)\right)\right)-155 \omega\right|=\left(\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}}\left(\mathrm{r} \cdot \sum_{\mathrm{p}=1}^{\mathrm{r}} \omega^{\mathrm{P}-1}\right)\right)-155 \omega$, then n is equal to -
questions.solution:
$\sum_{r=1}^{\mathrm{n}}\left(\mathrm{r} \cdot \sum_{\mathrm{p}=1}^{\mathrm{r}}\left(\omega^{\mathrm{P}-1}\right)\right)-155 \omega$
$=\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}}\left(\mathrm{r} \cdot\left(\omega^0+\omega^1+\omega^2+\omega^2+\ldots . .+\omega^{\mathrm{r}-1}\right)\right)-155 \omega$
$=1 \cdot \omega^0+2\left(\omega^0+\omega^0\right)+3\left(\omega^0+\omega^1+\omega^2\right)$
$+4\left(\omega^0+\omega^1+\omega^2+\omega^3\right) \ldots$.
$\mathrm{n}\left(\omega^0+\omega^1+\omega^2+\ldots+\omega^{4-1}\right)-155 \omega$
$=1+2\left(-\omega^2\right)+3(0)+4(1)+5\left(-\omega^2\right)+\ldots \ldots$.
upto n terms $-155 \omega$
will be real if $\mathrm{n}=29,30,31$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $u, v, w, z$ be complex number such that $|u| \lt 1,|v|=1$ and $w=\frac{v(u-z)}{\bar{u} z-1}$ then
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & |w|=\frac{|v||u-z|}{|\bar{u} z-1|}=\frac{|u-z|}{|\bar{u} z-1|} \\ & \text { Let, }|w| \leq 1 \\ & \Rightarrow|u-z| \leq|\bar{u} z-1| \\ & \Rightarrow(u-z)(\bar{u}-\bar{z}) \leq(u \bar{z}-1)(\bar{u} z-1) \\ & \Rightarrow\left(|u|^2-1\right)\left(|z|^2-1\right) \geq 1 \\ & \Rightarrow|z|^2-1 \leq 0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A complex number $z$ is rotated in anticlockwise direction by an angle $\alpha$ and we get $z^{\prime}$ and if the same complex number $z$ is rotated by an angle $\alpha$ in clockwise direction and we get $z^{\prime \prime}$ then
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & z^{\prime}=z e^{i \alpha}....(1) \\ & z^{\prime \prime}=z e^{-i \alpha}....(2) \\ & \therefore z^{\prime} z^{\prime \prime}=z^2 \Rightarrow z^{\prime}, z, z^{\prime \prime} \text { are in G.P. } \\ & \left(\frac{z^{\prime}}{z}\right)^2+\left(\frac{z^{\prime \prime}}{z}\right)^2=2 \cos 2 \alpha \\ & \Rightarrow z^{\prime 2}+z^{\prime \prime 2}=2 z^2 \cos 2 \alpha \\ & z^{\prime}+z^{\prime \prime}=2 z \cos \alpha\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For points $z_1, z_2, z_3, z_4$ in complex plane such that $\left|z_1\right| \lt 1,\left|z_2\right|=1$ and $\left|z_3\right| \leq 1$ and $z_3=\frac{z_2\left(z_1-z_4\right)}{z_1 z_4-1}$. then $\left|z_i\right|$ can be
questions.solution:
$z_3=\frac{z_2\left(z_1-z_4\right)}{\bar{z}_1 z_4-1}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow\left|z_3\right|=\frac{\left|z_2\right|\left|z_1-z_4\right|}{\left|\bar{z}_1 z_4-1\right|} \leq 1 \\ & \Rightarrow\left|z_1-z_4\right| \leq\left|\bar{z}_1 z_4-1\right| \\ & \Rightarrow\left|z_1\right|^2+\left|z_4\right|^2-z_1 \bar{z}_4-\overline{z_1} z_4 \leq\left|z_1\right|^2\left|z_4\right|^2-\bar{z}_1 z_4-z_1 \bar{z}_4+1 \\ & \Rightarrow\left|z_1\right|^2\left(1-\left|z_4\right|^2\right)-\left(1-\left|z_4\right|^2\right) \leq 0 \\ & \Rightarrow\left(\left|z_1\right|^2-1\right)\left(1-\left|z_4\right|^2\right) \leq 0 \\ & \Rightarrow 1-\left|z_4\right|^2\gt0 \\ & \Rightarrow\left|z_4\right| \lt 1\left(\because \mid z_1\right.\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $u, v, w, z$ be complex number such that $|u| \lt 1,|v|=1$ and $w=\frac{v(u-z)}{\bar{u} z-1}$ then
questions.solution:
$|\mathbf{w}|=\frac{|\mathbf{v}||\mathbf{u}-\mathrm{z}|}{|\overline{\mathbf{u}} \mathbf{z}-1|}=\frac{|\mathbf{u}-\mathrm{z}|}{|\overline{\mathbf{u}} \mathrm{z}-1|}$
Let. $|\mathbf{w}| \leq 1$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow|\mathrm{u}-\mathrm{z}| \leq|\overline{\mathrm{u}} \mathrm{z}-1| \\ & \Rightarrow(\mathrm{u}-\mathrm{z})(\overline{\mathrm{u}}-\overline{\mathrm{z}}) \leq(\mathrm{u} \overline{\mathrm{z}}-1)(\overline{\mathrm{u}} \mathrm{z}-1) \\ & \Rightarrow\left(|\mathrm{u}|^2-1\right)\left(|\mathrm{z}|^2-1\right) \geq 1 \\ & \Rightarrow|z|^2-1 \leq 0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $z_1=a+i b$ and $z_2=c+i d$ are two complex numbers $(a, b, c, d \in R)$ such that $\left|z_1\right|=\left|z_2\right|=1$ and $\operatorname{Im}\left(z_1 \bar{z}_2\right)=0$. If $w_1=a+i c$ and $w_2=b+i d$, then -
questions.solution:
Let $z_1=\cos \theta_1+i \sin \theta_1$ and $z_2=\cos \theta_2+i \sin \theta_2$ $\left(\because\left|z_1\right|=\left|z_2\right|=1\right)$
Now $\quad z_1 \bar{z}_2=\cos \left(\theta_1-\theta_2\right)+i \sin \left(\theta_1-\theta_2\right)$
$\operatorname{Im}\left(z_1 \bar{z}_2\right)=0 \Rightarrow \theta_1-\theta_2=\mathrm{n} \pi, \mathrm{n} \in \mathrm{I}$
Now $\mathrm{w}_1=\cos \theta_1+\mathrm{i} \cos \theta_2, \mathrm{w}_2=\sin \theta_1+\mathrm{i} \sin \theta_2$
(A) $\mathrm{w}_1 \overline{\mathrm{w}}_2=\cos \theta_1 \sin \theta_1+\cos \theta_2 \sin \theta_2+\mathrm{i} \sin \left(\theta_1-\theta_2\right)$
$\Rightarrow \quad \operatorname{Im}\left(w_1 \bar{w}_2\right)=0$
(B) $\mathrm{w}_2 \overline{\mathrm{w}}_1=\cos \theta_1 \sin \theta_1+\cos \theta_2 \sin \theta_2+\mathrm{i} \sin \left(\theta_1-\theta_2\right)$
$\Rightarrow \quad \operatorname{Im}\left(w_2 \bar{w}_1\right)=0$
(C) $\frac{\mathrm{w}_1}{\mathrm{w}_2}=\frac{\cos \theta_1+\mathrm{i} \cos \theta_2}{\sin \theta_1+\mathrm{i} \sin \theta_2} \times \frac{\sin \theta_1-\mathrm{i} \sin \theta_2}{\sin \theta_1-\mathrm{i} \sin \theta_2}$
$=\frac{\cos \theta_1 \sin \theta_1+\cos \theta_2 \sin \theta_2+i \sin \left(\theta_1-\theta_2\right.}{\sin ^2 \theta_1+\sin ^2 \theta_2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \operatorname{Im}\left(\frac{\mathrm{w}_1}{\mathrm{w}_2}\right)=0$
(D) Similarly $\operatorname{Re}\left(\frac{\mathrm{w}_1}{\overline{\mathrm{w}}_2}\right)=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $z=\exp \left(\operatorname{expi} \frac{\pi}{3}\right)$, then -
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{z}=\exp \left\{\frac{1}{2}+\mathrm{i} \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right\}=\mathrm{e}^{1 / 2} \cdot e^{\mathrm{i} \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}$
$z=e^{1 / 2}\left\{\cos \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}+i \sin \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right\}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \ell \mathrm{n}(\operatorname{Re} \mathrm{z})=\frac{1}{2}+\ell \mathrm{n}\left(\cos \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$
and $\quad \ln (\operatorname{Im} z)=\frac{1}{2}+\ln \left(\sin \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
